accurate.why: The claims made (short title, purpose, Title I one-third threshold, technology verification measures, one-year timeline, state preemption) are all supported by the bill text.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated or contradictory claims appear in the summary.

states_purpose.why: The summary clearly states the bill aims to protect minors online and empower parents.

changes_to_existing_law.why: The bill amends the Protecting Children in the 21st Century Act and references COPPA, but the summary (which cuts off) does not describe these amendments.

exceptions_conditions.why: The summary is truncated and fails to cover material conditions like the rule against requiring government ID or age-gating, and other carve-outs.

effective_dates.why: The summary notes the one-year Title I date but omits other timing provisions (180-day, 90-day, general 1-year effective date) due to truncation.

major_provisions_covered.why: The summary is cut off mid-sentence at Title II and omits Titles II-VI operative provisions (KOSA safeguards, messaging restrictions, gaming, chatbots, research, enforcement).

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary ends abruptly mid-sentence ('Title II regulates covered platforms that'), making it incomplete and not fully coherent.

concise.why: The summary avoids padding and filler.

correct_entities.why: The entities named (KIDS Act, platforms, social gaming providers, AI chatbot developers) are correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The one-third threshold and one-year enactment timeline stated are accurate per the bill.
